Recommendation stated (both before and after the offer); also genuinely about which L2-gated decision Brien wants to review, not L4 work the assistant should just execute.
---
Here it is. Seven open items (the header says "6" but lists 7 rows, that is the register drift I flagged in overwatch). Ages below are recomputed to today from the "surfaced" date, not the frozen 2026-07-03 build date.  | # | Item | Real age | L | Status right now | |---|------|---------:|---|------------------| | 1 | Parallax: rule on 8 three-axis refinements incl. 2 Wardley dissents (no non-Brien user anchor on Axis B) | 43d | L2 | **Open, actionable** | | 2 | Intent: ratify/reject 3 positioning DDRs (orchestration-spine, memory-altitude, verbatim-passthrough) | 43d | L2 | **Open, actionable** | | 3 | Layer 4.2 promote/retire + flight-model v1→v2 flip | 31d | L2 | **Parked, correctly**: blocked on the warn-only calibration window that closes 2026-07-18. Returns with real FP data then. | | 4 | Greenlight building the behavioral-setpoint control loop (drift sensor + conditional re-injection) | 28d | L2 | **Open, actionable** | | 5 | Repo-hygiene: rule on the proposed confidential-track control (fan-out convention change) | 11d | L2 | **Open, actionable** | | 6 | Loom: build the cross-session operation-declaration channel (status as liveness read-path) | 11d | L2 | **Open, actionable** | | 7 | Pulse: KEEP / PARK / mark-consumed. 2 weeks overdue; zero recorded consumption | 33d | L2 | **Resolvable today**: the 2026-07-13 cadence run is due today and should emit a real PARK recommendation. |  **Reading of the field:** - Two are not really waiting on you this instant. Row 3 is genuinely gated on the 07-18 window, and row 7 has a scheduled run today that will hand you a recommendation to react to rather than a cold call. I would not spend your attention on those two right now. - The real backlog is **five open L2 calls**, and they cluster into two arcs: **positioning** (rows 1, 2) and **enforcement-maturity / autonomy machinery** (rows 4, 5, 6). Deciding within an arc is faster than pinballing across them because they share context. - The two oldest (rows 1 and 2, both 43d) are the positioning arc. That is where the aging cost is highest: an unfiled positioning thesis leaves proposed_edges unwired and blocks the "commoditization-proof" narrative.  **My recommendation:** take the positioning arc first (rows 1 and 2 together, one sitting), since it is the oldest, self-contained, and unblocks downstream wiring. I can open both signals side by side and drive you through the actual choices. Want me to start there, or would you rather I pull up a different row, or fix the register drift (header count plus recompute the ages) first while you decide? I would take positioning first.
